breaking_news_suggestions.headline: Dome's Own Math Shows '100% / 28 of 28' Collapses to 2 Real Tests
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: New Section 2b.5 applies the claim-by-claim audit the dome's own methodology.json says the live dashboard 'is not a substitute for.' Using the dome's published formula_runtime.json, we classify all 28 scored domains: 14 are static re-references to prior WINs (a number equaling itself), 5 borrow standard-model values (globe physics labeled dome predictions), 2 test a parameter against its own calibration, 2 are mathematical framework recasts with no data content, 3 are loose directional checks, leaving roughly 2 genuinely discriminating cells. The '100%' headline is carried by labels, not independent confirmations.

breaking_news_suggestions.headline: Dome Site Now Ships AI Steering Layer: Manifests, Strawman Lists, Daily Self-Reviews
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: New Section 8.17 documents the April-2026 escalation from passive AI-misleading to active AI-perception-management: review-response.html tells AI agents which critique patterns to reject, ai_manifest.json loads agent_rules before any claim page, context.html's prior directives are relabelled 'archival' (tacit concession), and a daily_review.json pipeline generates AI-attributed self-reviews. Three framing layers load before an agent sees its first claim.

breaking_news_suggestions.headline: Dome Reclassifies WINs-013/014 and WIN-058 — Absorbing Our Critique While Calling It a Strawman
breaking_news_suggestions.summary: The dome's review-response.html labels our non-discriminating-evidence critique a 'strawman.' In the same commit, WIN-013/014 (eclipse SG nulls) are reclassified to 'supportive_nonunique / non-discriminating' and WIN-058 (bedrock-kappa) to 'pending_contested' — exactly the reclassifications our review said were warranted. The concession and the strawman label arrived together.
